focus: KQ8 FIX-5 criterion + KQ9 Claude flexibility + KQ10 first-dispatch verification
noveltyJustification: 2 of 6 findings are fully new, 3 partially new, and 1 mostly reframes prior evidence into a testable criterion.

summary: FIX-5 becomes mandatory if GPT-backed cli-opencode still fails the first-dispatch mode-local artifact/state/delta validation after agent-layer + pre-route edits while native/Claude baseline passes.
Preserve Claude's dynamic pre-dispatch routing/decomposition and retry/escalation flexibility; constrain only route/agent/prompt mismatches.
Preserve deep agents' evidence-responsive focus selection, ambiguity handling, source recovery, and verification-budget behavior.
Preserve advisory headers/hook hints as non-hard metadata and preserve AI Council depth-aware direct vs inline execution.
Existing workflow provenance is sufficient: state-log iteration records, artifact/delta post-dispatch validation, observability emission paths, and executor audit/failure records.
Minimal before/after test: one GPT-backed first dispatch per deep mode compared with native/Claude baseline, inspecting only existing JSONL/artifact/delta/provenance outputs.
